food_name: Zuppa del Giorno (Vegan Soup of the Day)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for 'Zuppa del Giorno' or Italian vegan soup of the day. FOODON_00002539 (vegetable soup) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Soups and broths) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for Italian vegan soup of the day.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226365003 (Vegetable soup) used as closest parent; no specific code for Italian vegan soup of the day.

allergens: gluten (possible, if pasta or bread included)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant bowl of Italian vegan soup of the day, approximately 300 g, as served at Ricciotti - The Riverwalk, Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Minestrone soup, canned, condensed — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 06932); adapted for vegan restaurant-style Italian vegetable soup
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for 'Zuppa del Giorno (Vegan)' as served at Ricciotti Singapore. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy minestrone/vegetable soup entries (NDB 06932, 06933) and cross-referenced with USDA Foundation Foods vegetable soup data. The dish is a rotating vegan Italian soup; nutrient values represent a plausible central estimate for a vegetable-broth-based Italian soup with seasonal vegetables, legumes, and possible pasta. Sodium is estimated at the lower end of restaurant soup ranges given the vegan, restaurant-quality context. All values are per 100 g of prepared soup.

gi_source.title: Minestrone soup GI — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283; University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for vegetable/minestrone soup.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for 'Zuppa del Giorno (Vegan)' as served at Ricciotti Singapore. GI of 38 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2008 International Tables (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care) for minestrone and vegetable-legume soups, which consistently report GI in the range 30–45. A central estimate of 38 is used. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (38 × 18.6) / 100 ≈ 7.1, where carbohydrate per 300 g serving = 6.2 × 3 = 18.6 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~280 mg per 100 g; ~840 mg per 300 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that a single 300 g restaurant serving may contribute approximately 840 mg sodium (~37% of the 2,300 mg daily limit), and should request reduced-salt preparation or limit portion size accordingly.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~38) and moderate carbohydrate content (~6.2 g per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low GI (approximately 38) and moderate glycemic load (~7 per serving) make this vegan soup a suitable choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, particularly when served without high-GI bread accompaniments.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.2 g per 100 g), zero cholesterol, and high vegetable content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This vegan soup is low in saturated fat and cholesterol-free, making it a heart-friendly option; however, the moderate sodium content warrants attention for individuals managing cardiovascular disease with sodium restriction.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Zuppa del Giorno (Vegan Soup of the Day) is an Italian-style rotating vegan soup served at Ricciotti - The Riverwalk in Singapore. The dish translates literally as 'soup of the day' and varies according to seasonal availability and the chef's selection. Typical preparations feature a clear or lightly thickened vegetable broth base enriched with olive oil, seasonal vegetables such as zucchini, tomatoes, carrots, celery, and onions, and may include legumes such as cannellini beans or lentils, and occasionally small pasta shapes or grains. The soup is simmered until vegetables are tender, yielding a light, savoury, and mildly herbed flavour profile with a thin to medium-bodied texture. As a vegan dish, it contains no animal products, dairy, or eggs. Nutritionally, it is low in energy (~42 kcal per 100 g), low in fat, and provides dietary fibre and plant-based micronutrients including vitamin A, vitamin C, iron, and folate. Its low glycemic index (~38) makes it suitable for blood glucose management. The sodium content (~280 mg per 100 g) is moderate and warrants attention for individuals on sodium-restricted diets. This dish is well suited as a starter or light main course and is appropriate for vegan, dairy-free, and halal dietary requirements.
common_user_queries: Is the soup of the day vegan at Ricciotti?
Is Italian soup of the day healthy?
Is zuppa del giorno gluten free?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists for 'Zuppa del Giorno (Vegan)' as served at Ricciotti Singapore in any approved primary source (HPB, USDA, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS). Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy minestrone and vegetable soup entries, which are the closest defensible analogues. The dish is a rotating menu item, so exact composition varies. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted estimates based on USDA SR Legacy minestrone/vegetable soup data (NDB 06932, 06933) and are not exact measurements for this specific rotating dish. 2. The dish composition varies daily; the record represents a plausible central estimate for a vegan Italian vegetable soup. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero for a plant-based olive-oil-prepared soup with no hydrogenated fats; confirmed zero per USDA source analogue. 4. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a fully vegan dish containing no animal products. 5. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero as vitamin B12 is not present in plant-based foods without fortification; no fortification is indicated for this dish. 6. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero for a non-fortified plant-based soup with no UV-exposed mushrooms indicated. 7. monounsaturated_fat_g (0.7), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.2), potassium_mg (180.0), calcium_mg (22.0), iron_mg (0.8), vitamin_c_mg (4.5), vitamin_a_mcg_rae (35.0), vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg (0.05), vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg (0.03), vitamin_b3_niacin_mg (0.5), vitamin_b6_mg (0.08), folate_mcg_dfe (12.0), vitamin_e_mg (0.5), phosphorus_mg (38.0), magnesium_mg (10.0), zinc_mg (0.3), selenium_mcg (1.2), water_g (89.5) are all estimated from USDA SR Legacy vegetable/minestrone soup analogues and are not individually confirmed for this specific dish. 8. GI of 38 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care) for minestrone and vegetable-legume soups. 9. The food_group in the input was listed as 'Grains and staples'; this has been corrected to 'Mixed' as the dish is a vegetable-based soup and not primarily a grain or starch product. 10. Multilingual names are provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this dish is served in Singapore. Italian is the cuisine of origin but the dish is not a Singaporean dish; multilingual names are provided as a best-practice addition for the Singapore serving context.
